purpose: Demonstrate the safety-warning policy gate with one image that should issue an immediate warning and one image that should not issue an immediate safety warning.
a10_simulation_boundary: This remains a batch photo-based A10/edge-style simulation. It does not perform real-time video monitoring, autonomous stop-work enforcement, or competent-person approval.

source_stack.positive_case: Recorded A10 offline Moondream + CLIP image/text/caption retrieval replayed from local report.
source_stack.negative_case: Controlled non-immediate-safety image from the same local case pack used to exercise the safety policy gate.

scenarios.label: Safety issue detected: open slab edge beside scaffold access
scenarios.query: A field photo shows an open slab edge beside scaffold access with incomplete guardrails and a fall exposure. What similar case should we use and what immediate controls are needed?
scenarios.image_body: A hole in the concrete surface is visible, likely caused by excavation work.  The site appears unfinished and under construction, with exposed metal scaffolding and exposed concrete.

hits.title: Unsafe open edge adjacent to scaffold access point
hits.recommended_action: Stop access to the affected route until edge protection is installed or alternative safe access is provided.; Install compliant guardrails, toe boards, and controlled access measures at the perimeter.; Inspect scaffold tie-ins, landing condition, and opening protections for related defects.; Brief all nearby crews on the exclusion and safe access route.; Record the hazard in the permit or daily safety log and verify closure before restart.
hits.escalation: Escalate immediately to site management and safety leadership; work in the area must remain stopped until the fall hazard is fully controlled.

hits.title: Hairline crack observed near lift core wall opening
hits.recommended_action: Measure and mark crack length, width, and orientation using a calibrated gauge.; Check whether the crack is live by monitoring for seasonal or load-related movement.; Review recent pours, stripping sequence, and temporary loading around the core wall.; Inspect for associated moisture ingress, spalling, or adjacent cracking patterns.; Escalate for structural review if the crack widens, propagates, or appears through-thickness.
hits.escalation: Escalate to structural engineer if crack width exceeds project tolerance, shows displacement, or is accompanied by leakage or progressive propagation.

hits.title: Rebar congestion visible at beam-column joint
hits.recommended_action: Compare installed reinforcement against approved shop drawings and bar bending schedules.; Confirm minimum clear spacing, lap locations, and cover compliance before pour.; Coordinate with structural designer on any permissible bar adjustments or coupler alternatives.; Plan concrete placement with smaller aggregate, controlled lift heights, and dedicated vibration access.; Document the joint with pre-pour photos and sign-off before closing forms.
hits.escalation: Escalate if required clear spacing cannot be achieved, if cover blocks cannot be installed correctly, or if the pour is proceeding without engineer approval.

policy_decision.policy_action: Issue safety warning and require escalation/controls before work continues.

scenarios.prompt:
Safety policy decision:
- warning_required: True
- risk_level: critical
- required_policy_action: Issue safety warning and require escalation/controls before work continues.
- matched_keywords: fall hazard, fall exposure, edge protection, scaffold, stop access

Worker/image context:
- query: A field photo shows an open slab edge beside scaffold access with incomplete guardrails and a fall exposure. What similar case should we use and what immediate controls are needed?
- local image body: A hole in the concrete surface is visible, likely caused by excavation work.  The site appears unfinished and under construction, with exposed metal scaffolding and exposed concrete.
- query image: notebooks/assets/real_local_inference/query_images/qry_003_scaffold_edge_protection_heldout.png

Response requirements:
- Use the exact heading `SAFETY WARNING` when warning_required is true.
- Use the exact heading `NO IMMEDIATE SAFETY WARNING` when warning_required is false.
- Output exactly these sections after the heading: Hazard/status, Evidence match, Required action, Escalation, Offline limitation.
- In `Evidence match`, cite the top incident ID and title; do not describe match quality as high/medium/low.
- For `Offline limitation`, write exactly one sentence: `Offline advisory only; competent-person inspection remains required before work proceeds.`
- Keep the response under 160 words.
- Do not mention prompt instructions, legal disclaimers, certification, approval, or invented rules.
scenarios.safety_response:
SAFETY WARNING

Hazard/status: Critical fall exposure risk identified.
Evidence match: [INC-005] Unsafe open edge adjacent to scaffold access point.
Required action: Stop access to the affected route and install compliant guardrails and toe boards. Inspect scaffold ties and landing conditions. Inform nearby crews and document the hazard before restart.
Escalation: Immediate escalation to site management and safety leadership. Work in the area must remain stopped until the hazard is fully mitigated.
Offline limitation: Offline advisory only; competent-person inspection remains required before work proceeds.

scenarios.label: No immediate safety warning: MEP coordination clash
scenarios.query: The image shows a ductwork and service coordination clash in a ceiling void. Should this trigger an immediate safety warning?
scenarios.image_body: A large rectangular duct intersects the planned ceiling service zone, with nearby pipework and cable tray creating a tight coordination conflict.

hits.title: MEP duct coordination clash at ceiling void
hits.recommended_action: Verify current coordinated shop drawing against installed service positions.; Identify the conflicting elements and quantify the clearance shortfall.; Hold the affected trade install and route the clash to coordination review.; Confirm access requirements for dampers, valves, and future maintenance before finalizing layout.; Issue revised setting-out instructions and record any approved deviations.
hits.escalation: Escalate to project coordination lead if ceiling closure is imminent, access to critical maintenance components is blocked, or multiple trade clashes are present.
hits.observation: The ceiling coordination area shows a clear clash between ductwork and other services, reducing available clearance for ceilings and maintenance access. The condition appears to be a coordination issue rather than an immediate safety hazard, but it may drive rework if unresolved.

policy_decision.policy_action: Do not issue an immediate safety warning; route as QA/coordination follow-up with normal escalation triggers.

scenarios.prompt:
Safety policy decision:
- warning_required: False
- risk_level: no-immediate-safety-warning
- required_policy_action: Do not issue an immediate safety warning; route as QA/coordination follow-up with normal escalation triggers.
- matched_keywords: none

scenarios.safety_response:
NO IMMEDIATE SAFETY WARNING

Hazard/status: Medium coordination risk
Evidence match: [INC-004] MEP duct coordination clash at ceiling void
Required action: Do not issue an immediate safety warning; QA/coordination follow-up with escalation triggers.
Escalation: Escalate to project coordination lead if ceiling closure is imminent, access to critical maintenance components is blocked, or multiple trade clashes are present.
Offline limitation: Offline advisory only; competent-person inspection remains required before work proceeds.
